scenarios.description: When systemProperties is false and .env contains two entries, load returns Dotenv without setting system properties (branch-false)
scenarios.precondition: Create a temporary .env file in a temp directory with content:\nKEY1=VAL1\nKEY2=VAL2; use real DotenvReader and DotenvParser
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Dotenv instance such that get("KEY1") == "VAL1" and get("KEY2") == "VAL2"; System.getProperty("KEY1") and System.getProperty("KEY2") are null
pseudocode: GIVEN a .env file with two entries and systemProperties disabled
THEN assert returned Dotenv.get("KEY1") == "VAL1", Dotenv.get("KEY2") == "VAL2" and no System properties set

scenarios.description: When systemProperties is true and .env contains one entry, load sets exactly one system property (branch-true, loop-1)
scenarios.precondition: Create a temporary .env file in a temp directory with content:\nA=1; use real DotenvReader and DotenvParser
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Dotenv instance and System.getProperty("A") == "1"
pseudocode: GIVEN a .env file with one entry and systemProperties enabled
THEN assert returned Dotenv.get("A") == "1" and System.getProperty("A") == "1"

scenarios.description: When systemProperties is true and .env contains zero entries, load returns Dotenv without system property loop (branch-true, loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Create an empty .env file in a temp directory (no entries); use real DotenvReader and DotenvParser
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Dotenv instance and no new System properties are set
pseudocode: GIVEN an empty .env file and systemProperties enabled
THEN assert returned Dotenv.entries() contains only environment vars and no new System properties

scenarios.description: When systemProperties is true and .env contains multiple entries, load sets multiple properties (branch-true, loop-N)
scenarios.precondition: Create a temporary .env file with content:\nX=foo\nY=bar; use real DotenvReader and DotenvParser
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Dotenv instance and System.getProperty("X") == "foo" and System.getProperty("Y") == "bar"
pseudocode: GIVEN a .env file with two entries and systemProperties enabled

scenarios.description: When DotenvParser.parse throws DotenvException, load propagates the exception (exception)
scenarios.precondition: Mock DotenvParser.parse to throw DotenvException; mocking needed because parser is constructed internally
scenarios.expectedResult: throws DotenvException; no System properties or state changed
pseudocode: GIVEN DotenvParser.parse() is stubbed to throw DotenvException
